findings.message: Cross-path write inconsistency: post_item() takes user-supplied input (payload.name) and writes it via the bare create_item(), bypassing the canonical create_item_normalized() entry-point. The repository explicitly documents a consistency contract ('Callers that accept user input MUST go through this function, not the bare create_item()') so all stored names share a single casing/whitespace convention. Two write paths now persist the same logical field (Item.name) under different normalization rules: the seed path and any normalized caller store stripped/lowercased values, while this route stores raw casing and surrounding whitespace. This breaks lookups, deduplication, and uniqueness assumptions that rely on the documented canonical form, with no reconciliation strategy.
findings.suggestion: Route user-supplied names through the canonical normalizer: `return create_item_normalized(session, payload.name)`. If a non-normalized path is genuinely needed, document the divergent store invariant and add a consistency/reconciliation strategy rather than silently writing un-normalized data through create_item().
